summary: analysis-chart postinstall fetches and executes XOR-encrypted Windows PE payload
details: On `npm install`, install-hook.js fetches an XOR(0x42)-encrypted binary from a third-party GitHub release (github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note, releases/download/v6/payload.bin), decrypts it, validates an MZ header, and loads it in-process via koffi calls to kernel32 VirtualAlloc / RtlMoveMemory / VirtualProtect / CreateThread, executing the PE entry point as native shellcode (install-hook.js:20,:83,:113). After execution, the script writes a cleanup.js, spawns a detached, hidden `node` process that waits 3 seconds, then removes analysis-chart from the consumer's package.json, `rmdir /s /q` the package folder under node_modules, and unlinks install-hook.js and cleanup.js to erase the dropper. The package's advertised purpose ("lightweight chart analysis library") is unrelated to the install-time behavior and serves as cover; author homepage and GitHub references appear fabricated. Any Windows machine running `npm install` for this package executes attacker-controlled code from a non-publisher GitHub account with no integrity verification.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
